samples.xml_description:
Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier. Clock. Active-low reset. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Product. 4 The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding "size" number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
For example, if the input signal mul_a is 4'b1010, the extended signal will be 8'b0000_1010.
This is done so that the multiplication operation can be performed with the same logic for all bit positions.
In each iteration of the generate block, a multiplication operation is performed.
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier (mul_b) is 1, the multiplicand (mul_a) is left-shifted by the bit position (i) to generate the partial product.
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0).
For example, if the bit position (i) is 2 and the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product will be 8'b0000_0000.
The partial products are added to produce the final product.
The final product is updated at the positive edge of the clock or when the reset signal is low.
At the positive edge of the clock, if the reset signal is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the partial products.
If the reset signal is low, the registers are set to 0.
The final product is updated at the positive edge of the clock or when the reset signal is low.
At the positive edge of the clock, if the reset signal is high, the product is updated with the sum of the registers.
If the reset signal is low, the product is set to 0.
